verdict: PARTIALLY REPAIRED. Three namespaces now first-class columns on agents (migration 0010). One inline-duplicate encoder eliminated. One bridge bug (AGENT://TNFCORE/) fixed. Three open follow-ups remaining.

canonicalEntityId.phase_9_outcome: DB column added (canonical_entity_id). Bridge now uses builder.

idNumber.phase_9_outcome: DB column added (id_number) with unique partial index. Seeder assigns deterministic values. TranscriptProcessorV2/V3/V4 now use shared helper from V2.

mcid.format: tnf/mcid/0.1 envelope (UUID correlation_id, causation_id, trace_id, scope.tenant_id)
mcid.phase_9_outcome: Listed in agents.federation->>'mcid'. NOT YET POPULATED by master-clock at registration time (only by relay envelope on message dispatch).

open_follow_ups.title: vector_id (ID#:hashbytes) shares prefix with idNumber
open_follow_ups.details: scripts/autonomy/generate_merkle_tree.py emits ID#:${base58.b58encode(hash[:8])} for KNOWLEDGE_TREE.json leaf vectors. These are intelligence-vector IDs, not federated reputation sequences. Three options: leave shared prefix, switch vector_id to VEC#, switch idNumber to FED#. Recommendation: leave for now; semantic distinction is preserved by content/auditability.

open_follow_ups.title: FederatedIdentityService increments do not flow back to agents.id_number
open_follow_ups.details: Live tnf register calls do not currently SELECT-then-UPDATE the agents row to record the assigned id_number. Bridge emits operationalHandle but not id_number. Production IDs are computed in-memory and stored in Redis only.
open_follow_ups.remediation: Add a follow-up hook in agent-registry-bridge.assignAgentId to ROUND trip id_number after first sequence allocation, gated on whether FederatedIdentityService is wired in the current process. Not blocking federation; blocking observability.

open_follow_ups.title: mcid bundle is not populated by master-clock on registration
open_follow_ups.details: agents.federation->>'mcid' is nullable. The mcid is generated per-envelope at message-dispatch time. Agents that register but never dispatch never get one.
open_follow_ups.remediation: Have master-clock emit a registration envelope that includes mcid { correlation_id, causation_id: null, trace_id } on each agent registration. Store on agents.federation->>'mcid'.

phase_9_fixes_applied.after: canonicalEntityId: buildCanonicalEntityId({ category: 'AGENT', provider: 'TNFCORE', name: AGENT_ID, instance: 1 })
phase_9_fixes_applied.root_cause: string-template emit that did not match normalizeCanonicalEntityId's segment[0]==='TNF' check

phase_9_fixes_applied.after: generateFederatedIdNumber(video.index) helper (exported, also imported by V3 and V4)
phase_9_fixes_applied.root_cause: had a commented-out canonical import; re-implemented locally instead

phase_9_fixes_applied.before: no canonicalEntityId, no idNumber, no federation populated
phase_9_fixes_applied.after: deterministic Base58 idNumber + builder-driven canonicalEntityId + scoped federation jsonb
phase_9_fixes_applied.root_cause: Phase 4 seeder wasn't aware of the federated identity field group

do_not_break: AGENT_REGISTER envelope payload shape (additive fields only)
Merkle root verification (additional optional fields do not affect hash)
next_move_recommended: Follow-up: wire FederatedIdentityService -> agents.id_number round trip (FOLLOWUP-2) and mcid on registration (FOLLOWUP-3) so the registry is the canonical home of all three namespaces, not just canonicalEntityId.
